tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows significant renal impairment (elevated creatinine, marked proteinuria), hypoglycemia with low HbA1c, and mild metabolic acidosis (low CO₂). Urinalysis reveals glucosuria, ketonuria, and abnormal urine appearance, suggesting possible tubular dysfunction or diabetic ketoacidosis, though HbA1c is low. Further clinical correlation and urgent nephrology and endocrinology evaluation are recommended.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
